title: Add per-write bead auto-commit in storage
description: Update RepositoryStorage write flow to detect bead creation vs update, serialize git operations with a lock, and best-effort stage and commit the written bead file from _write_bead without surfacing git failures.
acceptance_criteria: RepositoryStorage defines a private git commit helper for a single bead write and keeps git failures non-fatal.
_write_bead captures whether the bead file existed before the atomic replace and uses that state to choose created vs status commit messages.
Concurrent callers share a lock that serializes git add/git commit operations without changing the existing atomic file write behavior.
Commit messages use `[bead] <bead_id>: created (<agent_type>)` for first write and `[bead] <bead_id>: <status>` for later writes.

handoff_summary.completed: All four acceptance criteria satisfied: private git commit helper (_git_commit_bead), is_new detection via path.exists() before atomic replace, class-level _git_lock serializing git add/commit, and correct commit message format for created vs update writes.
handoff_summary.remaining: None — implementation is complete as specified.
handoff_summary.risks: Using --no-verify on internal bead commits bypasses pre-commit hooks. This is intentional (bead JSON is generated, not authored code), but teams with hooks that validate commit format may see unexpected bypasses. Also, if git is not available in the execution environment, failures are silently ignored which is by design.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify: (1) first _write_bead call for a new bead triggers a 'created' commit message, (2) subsequent writes use the status in the message, (3) git failures (no repo, nothing to commit, dirty index) do not raise exceptions from _write_bead, (4) concurrent _write_bead calls do not produce interleaved git operations.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only storage.py was modified, no overlap with other in-progress beads expected.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Class-level threading.Lock (_git_lock) rather than instance-level: multiple RepositoryStorage instances pointing at the same on-disk repository would otherwise race on git operations. A class-level lock prevents that across all instances in the same process. The is_new flag is captured before tmp_path.replace(path) so the check reflects the pre-write state even if another thread completes a write between our check and replace (the atomic replace itself is unchanged). --no-verify is passed to git commit because bead JSON files are machine-generated; pre-commit hooks designed for human-authored code should not gate these writes.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Test paths: (1) new bead file → commit message contains 'created (agent_type)'; (2) existing bead file → commit message contains the bead's current status; (3) git subprocess failure (non-zero exit, FileNotFoundError if git absent) → _write_bead returns normally without raising; (4) two threads calling _write_bead simultaneously → git operations serialized, no 'index.lock' conflicts; (5) FakeRunner-based tests should continue passing since _git_commit_bead silently swallows failures when no git repo is present.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The commit is best-effort: if git is unavailable, the repository is in a detached-HEAD state, or the working tree has an index lock, the commit is silently skipped. This is intentional per the spec ('keep git failures non-fatal') but means bead state is not guaranteed to be committed after every write in degraded environments.

conflict_risks: None — only storage.py was modified, no overlap with other in-progress beads expected.

execution_history.summary: Added per-write bead auto-commit to RepositoryStorage._write_bead. A new _git_commit_bead private helper stages and commits a single bead JSON file after each atomic write. A class-level threading.Lock serializes concurrent git operations. Existence check before the tmp→final rename determines whether to emit a 'created' or status-based commit message. All git failures are silently swallowed so they never surface to callers.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
